title: Convert ParseIntError with From
scenario: The parser has one low-level error that should become a ParseUserError. A From implementation makes that conversion reusable.
instructions: Implement From<ParseIntError> for ParseUserError by returning ParseUserError::InvalidId(error).

hints.body: From<T> defines fn from(value: T) -> Self.
The body only needs to wrap the ParseIntError in the InvalidId variant.
This is the conversion that lets ? work in later parser code.

completionExplanation: From centralizes the low-level to high-level error conversion. Call sites can ask for ParseUserError without rewriting the wrapping logic each time.
